meta.reason: Higher values of Clump Thickness and Cell Size Uniformity are often associated with malignancy.
Bare Nuclei and Bland Chromatin values above these thresholds are indicative of abnormal cell growth.
Low values in Single Epithelial Cell Size and Marginal Adhesion suggest benign characteristics.
Elevated Normal Nucleoli and Mitoses counts are strong indicators of cancerous activity.
Low Cell Shape Uniformity and Clump Thickness are typically associated with benign tumors.
Bare_Nuclei and Bland_Chromatin are indicators of abnormal cell growth when they have higher values.
Lower values of Marginal_Adhesion and Single_Epi_Cell_Size can suggest benign conditions.
Elevated Normal_Nucleoli and Mitoses counts are often linked to aggressive tumor behavior.
Low Cell_Shape_Uniformity and Clump_Thickness are typically associated with benign tumors.
Increased nucleoli and mitotic activity suggest aggressive cell proliferation.
Elevated Bare Nuclei and Bland Chromatin scores can indicate abnormal cell growth.
Low values in Single Epithelial Cell Size and Marginal Adhesion suggest benign characteristics.
Presence of many bare nuclei or increased mitotic activity can indicate malignancy.
Lower cell shape uniformity and marginal adhesion are typically seen in benign cells.
Larger epithelial cell size or more bland chromatin can suggest abnormal cell growth.
Few nucleoli and low mitotic count are common in benign conditions.
